interactions.summary: Jiang compares university teaching about Satan's deception with Genesis itself, arguing that the serpent tells the truth: eating the fruit opens the eyes and God banishes Adam and Eve to prevent immortality.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang reading Genesis and interpreting Satan's speech.

interactions.summary: He explains Eve's certainty through the inner light connected to the Monad, then returns to Nephilim as the Bible's transformation of polytheistic heroes into sons-of-God propaganda.
Jiang asks for final questions; this is a lecturer prompt and not a public source question by itself.

claims.claim: Jiang contrasts his Yale education, which taught Satan as clever liar and Eve as foolish, with his claim that Genesis itself shows the serpent telling the truth.
Genesis is read as saying Adam and Eve did not die from the fruit; their eyes opened and they gained knowledge, so Satan's promise was true.
Jiang distinguishes punishment from banishment: labor pain and hard agricultural work are the punishment, while banishment prevents access to the tree of life.
The two trees are defined as the tree of knowledge, which gives the mind of God, and the tree of life, which gives life as long as God.
Eve knows Satan is telling the truth because the inner light, connected to the Monad, tells the person what is false and true.
Jiang reads the Nephilim passage as the Bible turning polytheistic heroes such as Hercules, Achilles, and Theseus into offspring of old gods under a monotheistic frame.
The Bible is characterized as a tremendous act of propaganda that reinvents reality around one true God while denying intuition and human history.

signature_moments.moment: The professor says Satan lies; Jiang says read the Bible yourself and Satan is telling the truth.
signature_moments.source_phrase: When you actually read the Bible by yourself, you will see he's telling the truth
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the lecture's direct confrontation with institutional interpretation.

signature_moments.moment: The banishment is not the punishment; it is containment after humans become nearly divine.
signature_moments.source_phrase: The banishment is not the punishment
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It flips Eden from morality tale into prison-security logic.

signature_moments.moment: Eve knows because the light inside her still speaks from the Monad.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It links the Eden scene back to the lecture's inner-divinity model.

signature_moments.moment: Hercules becomes Nephilim and the Bible becomes propaganda against polytheistic memory.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns mythological heroes into casualties of monotheistic rewriting.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The Eden tree Jiang says gives the mind of God or knowledge of good and evil.
The Eden tree Jiang says would let humans live as long as God.
The connection to the Monad inside the person that can recognize truth against the false God.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang says the class will continue the Nephilim/Bible-as-propaganda theme throughout the semester.
